title: Auto-Match Migration Fields by Case-Insensitive Name Comparison
story: As a user mapping migration fields, I want an auto-match button that automatically maps identical fields between source and Salesforce, so that I can save hundreds of clicks on obvious matches.
description:
Add an "Auto-Match" checkbox to the migration field mapping screen. When checked, the save button changes to "Save Fields and Auto-Match" and attempts to automatically map source fields to Salesforce fields using case-insensitive, space-removed string comparison.

### Matching Logic
1. Lowercase both source and Salesforce field names
2. Remove spaces from both
3. Compare source field name against Salesforce field API name
4. Compare source field name against Salesforce field label
5. Only match against fields with "Not Reviewed" status
6. Skip fields that already have a migration status or mapping
7. Exact match only — no fuzzy matching

### UX
- Checkbox above the save button: "Auto-Match"
- When checked, button text changes to "Save Fields and Auto-Match"
- Results show matched fields with updated status

### Reusability
The auto-match function should be reusable for picklist value mapping as well.
acceptance_criteria: Auto-Match checkbox is available above the save button
Checking the box changes button text to Save Fields and Auto-Match
Matches against both Salesforce field name and field label
Only Not Reviewed fields are considered for matching
No fuzzy matching — exact match only after normalization
Function is reusable for picklist value auto-matching
ux_changes: New Auto-Match checkbox and dynamic save button label. Matched fields update visually after auto-match runs.
middleware_changes: Backend auto-match function compares normalized field names and creates mapping records for exact matches, filtering to Not Reviewed status only.
implementation_notes: Matching normalizes by lowercasing and removing spaces. Developer confirmed the approach: 'First Name' matches 'FirstName' after normalization. 'Master Record' does NOT match 'MasterRecordID' — no fuzzy or partial matching.
comment_summary: Developer needed a meeting to clarify the feature. After discussion, confirmed the normalization approach (lowercase + remove spaces) and that only exact matches are mapped. Implementation was completed on staging with the Not Reviewed filter applied.
